q:
$$
\int \frac{2 x+5}{\sqrt{7-6 x-x^2}} d x=A \sqrt{7-6 x-x^2}+B \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+3}{4}\right)+C
$$
(where $C$ is a constant of integration), then the ordered pair $(A, B)$ is equal to
question:
$$
\int \frac{2 x+5}{\sqrt{7-6 x-x^2}} d x=A \sqrt{7-6 x-x^2}+B \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+3}{4}\right)+C
$$
(where $C$ is a constant of integration), then the ordered pair $(A, B)$ is equal to
solution:
$\because 7-6 x-x^2=16-(x+3)^2$
and $\frac{d}{d x}\left(7-6 x-x^2\right)=-2 x-6$
So, $\int \frac{2 x+5}{\sqrt{7-6 x-x^2}} d x=\int \frac{2 x+6}{\sqrt{7-6 x-x^2}} d x$
$$
-\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{16-(x+3)^2}} d x
$$

$$
=-2 \sqrt{7-6 x-x^2}-\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+3}{4}\right)+C
$$
Therefore, $A=-2, \& B=-1$.

x-1-x+2x-12.dx = dt

subject: Mathematics
q: If $f(x)=\int\left(\frac{x^2+\sin ^2 x}{1+x^2}\right) \sec ^2 x d x$ and $f(0)=0$, then $f(1)$ equals
question: If $f(x)=\int\left(\frac{x^2+\sin ^2 x}{1+x^2}\right) \sec ^2 x d x$ and $f(0)=0$, then $f(1)$ equals
solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Let } f(x)=\int\left(\frac{x^2+\sin ^2 x}{1+x^2}\right) \sec ^2 x d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2 \sec ^2 x+\frac{\sin ^2 x}{\cos ^2 x}}{1+x^2} d x
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\int \frac{x^2 \sec ^2 x+\tan ^2 x}{1+x^2} d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2\left(1+\tan ^2 x\right)+\tan ^2 x}{1+x^2} d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2+\tan ^2 x\left(1+x^2\right)}{1+x^2} d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2}{1+x^2} d x+\int \tan ^2 x d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2+1-1}{1+x^2} \mathrm{dx}+\int\left(\sec ^2 \mathrm{x}-1 \mathrm{dx}\right. \\
& =\int 1 d x-\int \frac{d x}{1+x^2}+\int \sec ^2 x d x-\int d x \\
& =-\tan -1 x+\tan ^{-1} x+\mathrm{c} \\
& \text { Given: } f(0)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow f(0)=-\tan ^{-1} 0+\tan ^2+c \\
& \Rightarrow c=0 \\
& \therefore f(x)=-\tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^2 x
\end{aligned}
$$
Now,
$$
f(1)=-\tan ^{-1}(1)+\tan 1=\tan 1-\frac{\pi}{4}
$$

∫(2x-1)cos(2x-1)2+5(2x-1)2+5dx

22x-1 2dx=2 tdt

2t2-5dx=tdt

So ∫t2-5cost2t2-5dt=12sint+c

=12sin(2x-1)2+5+c

solution:
∫x2-1dxx4+3x2+1tan-1x+1x+∫dxx4+3x2+1

∫1-1x2dxx+1x2+1tan-1x+1x+12∫x2+1-x2-1dxx4+3x2+1

Put tan-1x+1x=t

∫dtt+12∫1+1x2dxx-1x2+5-12∫1-1x2dxx+1x2+1

Put x-1x=y,x+1x=z

loget+12∫dyy2+5-12∫dzz2+1

=logetan-1x+1x+125tan-1x2-15x

-12tan-1x2+1x+C

α=1,β=125,γ=15,δ=-12

or α=1,β=-125,γ=-15,δ=-12

10(α+βγ+δ)=101+110-12=6

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>3</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mn>5</mn><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>4</mn></mfenced></math> is equal to

solution:
Let

I=∫2xx2+1x2+3dx

Put x2=t⇒2xdx=dt

I=∫1t+1t+3dt

⇒I=12∫2t+1t+3dt

⇒I=12∫1t+1-1t+3dt

⇒I=12lnt+1-lnt+3+C

⇒fx=12lnx2+1-lnx2+3+C

Put x=3, then

12ln5-ln6=12ln10-ln12+C

12ln5-ln6=12ln2+ln5-ln2-ln6+C

⇒C=0

So,

fx=12lnx2+1-lnx2+3

⇒f4=12ln17-ln19 or f4=12loge17-loge19

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $f(x)=\int \frac{\left(2-x^{2}\right) \cdot \mathrm{e}^{x}}{(\sqrt{1+x})(1-x)^{3 / 2}} \mathrm{~d} x$. If $f(0)=0$, then $f\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$ is equal to:
question: Let $f(x)=\int \frac{\left(2-x^{2}\right) \cdot \mathrm{e}^{x}}{(\sqrt{1+x})(1-x)^{3 / 2}} \mathrm{~d} x$. If $f(0)=0$, then $f\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$ is equal to:
solution:
Write $2 - x^2 = (1-x)(1+x) + 1$, so the integrand splits as $e^x\left(\sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}} + \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+x}(1-x)^{3/2}}\right)$.
Let $g(x) = \sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}}$. Then $g'(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+x}(1-x)^{3/2}}$.
Using $\int e^x(g + g')dx = e^x g + C$: $f(x) = e^x\sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}} + C$.
$f(0) = 1 + C = 0 \Rightarrow C = -1$.
$f(1/2) = e^{1/2}\sqrt{\frac{3/2}{1/2}} - 1 = \sqrt{e}\cdot\sqrt{3} - 1 = \sqrt{3e} - 1$.

I=∫sin-1x1+xdx

=∫tan-1x⏟I1⏟IIdx

=xtan-1x-∫11+x.12x.xdx+C

=xtan-1x-12∫t.2t.dt1+t2+C (putting x=t2⇒dx=2tdt)

Comparing with Axtan-1x+Bx+C, we get

solution:
Let

I=∫0∞6e3x+6e2x+11ex+6dx

⇒I=∫0∞6ex+1ex+2ex+3dx

Using partial fraction, we can write

⇒I=6∫0∞12ex+1-1ex+2+12ex+3dx

⇒I=6∫0∞12e-x+1-11+2e-x+121+3e-xe-xdx

⇒I=6-12logee-x+1+12loge2e-x+1-16loge3e-x+10∞

⇒I=60--12loge2+12loge3-16loge4

⇒I=3loge2-3loge3+loge4

⇒I=5loge2-3loge3

⇒I=loge3227

question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>x</mi><mo>≠</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math>, and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>A</mi><mi>log</mi><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>B</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi></math> , then the ordered pair <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>B</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given f3x-43x+4=x+2, x≠-43

Let,  3x-43x+4=t

3x-4=3tx+4t

x=4t+43-3t+2

∴ft=10-2t3-3t

⇒fx=2x-103x-3

∫fxdx=∫2x-103x-3dx

=∫2x3x-3dx-10∫dx3x-3

=2x3-83lnx-1+C, where C is the constant of integration.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let $f(x)$ be an indefinite integral of $\cos ^3 x$.
Statement 1: $f(x)$ is a periodic function of period
$\pi$.
Statement 2: $\cos ^3 x$ is a periodic function.
question:
Let $f(x)$ be an indefinite integral of $\cos ^3 x$.
Statement 1: $f(x)$ is a periodic function of period
$\pi$.
Statement 2: $\cos ^3 x$ is a periodic function.
options: Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is false.
Both the Statements are true, but Statement 2 is not the correct explanation of Statement 1.
Both the Statements are true, and Statement 2 is correct explanation of Statement 1.
Statement 1 is false, Statement 2 is true.
solution:
Statement $-2: \cos ^3 x$ is a periodic function. it is a true statement.
Statement-1
Given $f(x)=\int \cos ^3 x d x$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\int\left(\frac{\cos 3 x}{4}+\frac{3 \cos x}{4}\right) d x \\
& =\frac{1}{4} \frac{\sin 3 x}{3}+\frac{3}{4} \sin x \\
& =\frac{1}{12} \sin 3 x+\frac{3}{4} \sin x
\end{aligned}
$$
Now, period of $\frac{1}{12} \sin 3 x=\frac{2 \pi}{3}$
Period of $\frac{3}{4} \sin x=2 \pi$
Hence period of $f(x)=\frac{\text { L.C.M. }(2 \pi, 2 \pi}{\text { HCF of }(1,3)}$

$$
=\frac{2 \pi}{1}=2 \pi
$$
Thus, $f(x)$ is a periodic function of period $2 \pi$.
Hence, Statement $-1$ is false.

subject: Mathematics
q: The integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>cot</mi><mi>x</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>cosec</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cot</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></msqrt><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>x</mi><mo><</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo></math>is equal to
question: The integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>cot</mi><mi>x</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>cosec</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cot</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></msqrt><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>x</mi><mo><</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo></math>is equal to
solution:
Let I=∫1+2cotxcosec x+cotxdx  

⇒I=∫2cotxcosecx+cosec2x+cot2xdx

⇒I=∫cosec x+cot x2dx

⇒I=∫cosec x+cot xdx

⇒I=∫cosec x+cotxdx

⇒I=logtanx2+logsinx+c, where c is the constant of integration.
⇒I=logsinx2cosx2·2sinx2cosx2+c
⇒I=2logsinx2+c

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>5</mn></msup><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></msup><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>g</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>c</mi></math> is a constant of integration, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>5</mn></msup><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></msup><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>g</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>c</mi></math> is a constant of integration, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Let I=∫x5e-x2dx

Let, -x2=t⇒xdx=-12dt

∴ I=-12∫t2etdt

By using integrating by parts, i.e. ∫u·vdx=u∫vdx-∫ddxu∫vdxdx+c, we get

I=-12t2∫etdt-∫2t∫etdtdt+c

⇒I=-12t2et-2∫tetdt+c

Again, applying integrating by parts, we get

⇒I=-12t2et-2t∫etdt-∫1·etdt+c

⇒I=-12t2et-2tet-et+c

⇒I=-12t2et-2tet+2et+c

⇒I=-et2t2-2t+2+c

⇒I=-e-x22x4+2x2+2+c

Given, I=∫x5e-x2dx=gxe-x2+c

⇒gx=-12x4+2x2+2

⇒g-1=-12-14+2-12+2

⇒g-1=-52.

subject: Mathematics
q: If $\int \frac{x^2-x+1}{x^2+1} e^{\cot ^{-1} x} d x=A(x) e^{\cot ^{-1} x}+C$, then $A(x)$ is equal to :
question: If $\int \frac{x^2-x+1}{x^2+1} e^{\cot ^{-1} x} d x=A(x) e^{\cot ^{-1} x}+C$, then $A(x)$ is equal to :
solution:
Let $\mathrm{I}=\int \frac{x^2-x+1}{x^2+1} \cdot e^{\cot ^{-1} x} d x$
Put $x=\cot t \Rightarrow-\operatorname{cosec}^2 t d t=d x$
Now, $1+\cot ^2 t=\operatorname{cosec}^2 t$
$\therefore$
$\mathrm{I}=\int \frac{e^t\left(\cot ^2 t-\cot t+1\right)}{\left(1+\cot ^2 t\right)}\left(-\operatorname{cosec}^2 t\right) d t$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =-\int e^t\left(\operatorname{cosec}{ }^2 t-\cot t\right) d t \\
& =\int e^t\left(\cot t-\operatorname{cosec}^2 t\right) d t \\
& =e^t \cot t+\mathrm{C} \\
& =e^{\cot ^{-1} x}(x)+\mathrm{C} \equiv \mathrm{A}(x) \cdot e^{\cot ^{-1} x}+\mathrm{C} \\
\Rightarrow \mathrm{A}(x) & =x
\end{aligned}
$$

solution:
We have, I=∫sin5x2sinx2dx  

=∫sin2x+x2sinx2dx

Using sinA+B=sinAcosB+cosAsinB

=∫sin2xcosx2+cos2xsinx2sinx2dx

=∫2 sinx cosxcosx2sinx2+cos 2xdx

=∫22sinx2cosx2cosx cosx2sinx2+cos 2xdx

=∫4cos2x2cosx+cos2xdx

Using  cos2x=2cos2x-1⇒2cos2x=1+cos2x

=∫21+cosxcosx+cos2xdx

=∫2cosx+2cos2x+cos2xdx

=∫2cosx+1+cos2x+cos2xdx

=∫2cosx+2cos2x+1dx

=2sinx+sin2x+x+c, where c is the constant of integration.

solution:
sin8x-cos8x=sin2x-cos2xsin2x+cos2xsin4x+cos4x

and sin4x+cos4x=sin2x+cos2x2-2sin2xcos2x=1-2sin2xcos2x

∴I=∫sin8x-cos8x1-2sin2xcos2xdx

=∫sin2x-cos2xsin4x+cos4xsin4x+cos4xdx

 =-∫cos2xdx 

  =-12sin2x+c, where c is the constant of integration.

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>6</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup><mi> </mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>6</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> is a constant of integration, then the function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>6</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup><mi> </mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>6</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> is a constant of integration, then the function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
We have, ∫dxx31+x623=xfx1+x613+C

Now, I=∫dxx71x6+123

Put, t=1x6+1⇒dt=-6x7dx

∴I=-16∫dtt23=-12t13+C, where C is the constant of integration=-121x6+113+C=-121+x613x2+C

∴fx=-12x3

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><msup><mi>c</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then a possible choice of <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><msup><mi>c</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then a possible choice of <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
solution:
∫esec⁡x(sec⁡xtan⁡xfx+sec⁡xtan⁡x+sec2⁡xdx=esec⁡xfx+C

Differentiating both sides w.r.t ‘x’ we get

esecxsecxtanxfx+secxtanx+sec2x=esecx⋅secxtanxf(x)+esecxf'x

⇒f'x=sec2x+tanxsecx

⇒fx=∫sec2x+tanxsecxdx

⇒fx=tanx+secx+c, c∈R
Hence, possible choice is fx=secx+tanx+12.

subject: Mathematics
q: The integral $\int x \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right) d x(x>0)$ is equal to:
question: The integral $\int x \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right) d x(x>0)$ is equal to:
solution:
Let $\mathrm{I}=\int x \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right) d x$
$$
\therefore \mathrm{I}=2 \int_{\text {II }} x \cdot \tan ^{-1} x d x
$$
Applying Integration by parts
$$
\begin{array}{r}
\mathrm{I}=2\left[\tan ^{-1} x \int x d x-\int\left(\frac{d}{d x}\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right) \int x d x\right) d x\right] \\
\mathrm{I}=2\left[\frac{x^2}{2} \tan ^{-1} x-\int \frac{1}{1+x^2} \times \frac{x^2}{2} d x\right]+c \\
\mathrm{I}=2\left[\frac{x^2}{2} \tan ^{-1} x-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{x^2+1-1}{x^2+1} d x\right]+c \\
\mathrm{I}=2\left[\frac{x^2}{2} \tan ^{-1} x-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{x^2+1}{x^2+1} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{1+x^2} d x\right]+c
\end{array}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{I} &=2\left[\frac{x^2}{2} \tan ^{-1} x-\frac{1}{2} \int 1 . d x+\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x\right]+c \\
\mathrm{I} &=2\left[\frac{x^2}{2} \tan ^{-1} x-\frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x\right]+c \\
\mathrm{I} &=x^2 \tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} x-x+c \\
& \text { or } I=-x+\left(x^2+1\right) \tan ^{-1} x+c
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Mathematics
q:
If the integral
$$
\int \frac{\cos 8 x+1}{\cot 2 x-\tan 2 x} d x=A \cos 8 x+k
$$
where $k$ is an arbitrary constant, then $\mathrm{A}$ is equal to:
question:
If the integral
$$
\int \frac{\cos 8 x+1}{\cot 2 x-\tan 2 x} d x=A \cos 8 x+k
$$
where $k$ is an arbitrary constant, then $\mathrm{A}$ is equal to:
solution:
Let $\mathrm{I}=\int \frac{\cos 8 x+1}{\cot 2 x-\tan 2 x} d x$
Now, $\mathrm{D}^r=\cot 2 x-\tan 2 x=\frac{\cos 2 x}{\sin 2 x}-\frac{\sin 2 x}{\cos 2 x}$
$$
=\frac{\cos ^2 2 x-\sin ^2 2 x}{\sin 2 x \cos 2 x}=\frac{2 \cos 4 x}{\sin 4 x}
$$
$\begin{aligned} \therefore \mathrm{I} &=\int \frac{2 \cos ^2 4 x}{\frac{2 \cos 4 x}{\sin 4 x}} d x=\int \frac{2 \cos ^2 4 x \cdot \sin 4 x}{2 \cos 4 x} d x \\ &=\frac{1}{2} \int \sin 8 x d x=-\frac{1}{2} \frac{\cos 8 x}{8}+k \\ &=-\frac{1}{16} \cdot \cos 8 x+k \\ & \text { Now },-\frac{1}{16} \cdot \cos 8 x+k=\mathrm{A} \cos 8 x+k \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{A}=-\frac{1}{16} \end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><msup><mi>e</mi><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></msup><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>g</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>c</mi></math> is a constant of integration, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo></math> is
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><msup><mi>e</mi><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></msup><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>g</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>c</mi></math> is a constant of integration, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo></math> is
solution:
I=∫e2x+2ex-e-x-1eex+e-xdx

⇒I=∫e2x+ex-1eex+e-xdx+∫ex-e-xeex+e-xdx

⇒I=∫ex+1-e-xeex+e-x+xdx+eex+e-x+c

ex+e-x+x=u

ex-e-x+1dx=du

I=eex+e-x+x+eex+e-x+c=eex+e-xex+1+c

then g(x)=ex+1

g(0)=2

subject: Mathematics
q: If $\int \frac{d x}{x+x^7}=p(x)$ then, $\int \frac{x^6}{x+x^7} d x$ is equal to:
question: If $\int \frac{d x}{x+x^7}=p(x)$ then, $\int \frac{x^6}{x+x^7} d x$ is equal to:
solution:
$\int \frac{x^6}{x+x^7} d x=\int \frac{x^6}{x\left(1+x^6\right)} d x$
$=\int \frac{\left(1+x^6\right)-1}{x\left(1+x^6\right)} d x$
$=\int \frac{1}{x} d x-\int \frac{1}{x+x^7} d x$
$=\ln |x|-p(x)+c$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\mathrm{I}(x)=\int \frac{d x}{(x-11)^{\frac{11}{13}}(x+15)^{\frac{15}{13}}}$. If $\mathrm{I}(37)-\mathrm{I}(24)=\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{~b}^{\frac{1}{13}}}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{c}^{\frac{1}{13}}}\right), \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c} \in \mathrm{N}$, then $3(\mathrm{~b}+\mathrm{c})$ is equal to
question: Let $\mathrm{I}(x)=\int \frac{d x}{(x-11)^{\frac{11}{13}}(x+15)^{\frac{15}{13}}}$. If $\mathrm{I}(37)-\mathrm{I}(24)=\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{~b}^{\frac{1}{13}}}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{c}^{\frac{1}{13}}}\right), \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c} \in \mathrm{N}$, then $3(\mathrm{~b}+\mathrm{c})$ is equal to
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & I(x)=\int \frac{d x}{(x-11)^{\frac{11}{13}}(x+15)^{\frac{15}{13}}} \\ & \text { Put } \frac{x-11}{x+15}=t \Rightarrow \frac{26}{(x+5)^2} d x=d t \\ & I(x)=\frac{1}{26} \int \frac{d t}{t^{1 / 13}}=\frac{1}{26} \cdot \frac{t^{2 / 13}}{2 / 13}\end{aligned}$


$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{I}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}-11}{\mathrm{x}+15}\right)^{2 / 13}+\mathrm{C} \\ & \mathrm{I}(37)-\mathrm{I}(24)=\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{26}{52}\right)^{2 / 13}-\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{13}{39}\right)^{2 / 13} \\ & =\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{1}{2^{2 / 13}}-\frac{1}{3^{2 / 13}}\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{1}{4^{1 / 13}}-\frac{1}{9^{1 / 13}}\right) \\ & \therefore \mathrm{b}=4, \mathrm{c}=9 \\ & 3(\mathrm{~b}+\mathrm{c})=39\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>θ</mi></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>sec</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>θ</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi></math> where <math><mi>C</mi></math> is a constant of integration, then the ordered pair <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>λ</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>θ</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to:
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>θ</mi></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>sec</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>θ</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi></math> where <math><mi>C</mi></math> is a constant of integration, then the ordered pair <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>λ</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>θ</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to:
solution:
∫sec2θ1+tan2θ1-tan2θ+2tanθ1-tan2θdθ

=∫sec2θ1-tan2θ1+tanθ2dθ

=∫sec2θ1-tanθ1+tanθdθ

Let tanθ=t⇒sec2θdθ=dt

=∫1-t1+tdt=∫-1+21+tdt

=-t+2ln1+t+C

=-tanθ+2ln1+tanθ+C

⇒λ=-1 and fθ=1+tanθ

subject: Mathematics
q: The integral of $\frac{x^2-x}{x^3-x^2+x-1}$ w.r.t. $x$ is
question: The integral of $\frac{x^2-x}{x^3-x^2+x-1}$ w.r.t. $x$ is
solution:
Let $I=\int \frac{x^2-x}{x^3-x^2+x-1} d x$
$$
=\int \frac{x(x-1)}{x^2(x-1)+(x-1)} d x=\int \frac{x d x}{x^2+1}
$$

$$
\left.=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 x d x}{\left(x^2+1\right.}\right)
$$
Let $x^2+1=t \Rightarrow 2 x d x=d t$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore I=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d t}{t}=\frac{1}{2} \log t+c \\
& =\frac{1}{2} \log \left(x^2+1\right)+c
\end{aligned}
$$
where ' $c$ ' is the constant of integration.
